Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation

Book overview and chapter-level study-note index for Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation.

Book notes

Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation

Chapter-level study notes on Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation (Newcomer, Hatry, and Wholey 2015). These notes are generated from source-linked MCP research-library records and are intended for study, comparison, and later refinement.

31 chapter notes 2015 Source status: needs_review MCP evaluation-texts

Synthesis

Core Concepts

Recurring ideas and distinctions used across these chapter notes.

Map

Chapter Map

Source chapters, page ranges, and reading routes.

Practice

Practice Implications

Practical evaluation design habits drawn from the notes.

Source

2015MCP evaluation-textsStatus: needs_reviewBook overview

This index summarises generated chapter-level study notes for Kathryn E. Newcomer, Harry P. Hatry, and Joseph S. Wholey’s Handbook of Practical Program Evaluation (Newcomer, Hatry, and Wholey 2015). The generated pages use selected MCP research-library retrieval records and compact evidence summaries.

Core Argument

Practical evaluation improves programs when evaluators align design, data collection, analysis, reporting, contracting, and use with real information needs and credible evidence standards.

Chapter Notes

Part 1: Evaluation Planning and Design

  • Chapter 1: Planning and Designing Useful Evaluations - source pages 49-77
  • Chapter 2: Analyzing and Engaging Stakeholders - source pages 78-103
  • Chapter 3: Using Logic Models - source pages 104-129
  • Chapter 4: Exploratory Evaluation - source pages 130-149
  • Chapter 5: Performance Measurement - source pages 150-178
  • Chapter 6: Comparison Group Designs - source pages 179-201
  • Chapter 7: Randomized Controlled Trials - source pages 202-224
  • Chapter 8: Conducting Case Studies - source pages 225-248
  • Chapter 9: Recruitment and Retention of Study Participants - source pages 249-267
  • Chapter 10: Designing, Managing, and Analyzing Multisite Evaluations - source pages 268-299
  • Chapter 11: Evaluating Community Change Programs - source pages 300-322
  • Chapter 12: Culturally Responsive Evaluation - source pages 323-357

Part 2: Practical Data Collection Procedures

  • Chapter 13: Using Agency Records - source pages 367-384
  • Chapter 14: Using Surveys - source pages 385-424
  • Chapter 15: Role Playing - source pages 425-453
  • Chapter 16: Using Ratings by Trained Observers - source pages 454-487
  • Chapter 17: Collecting Data in the Field - source pages 488-518
  • Chapter 18: Using the Internet - source pages 519-537
  • Chapter 19: Semi-Structured Interviews - source pages 538-551
  • Chapter 20: Focus Group Interviewing - source pages 552-576
  • Chapter 21: Using Stories in Evaluation - source pages 577-597

Part 3: Data Analysis

  • Chapter 22: Analyzing Qualitative Data - source pages 603-635
  • Chapter 23: Using Statistics in Evaluation - source pages 636-677
  • Chapter 24: Cost-Effectiveness and Cost-Benefit Analysis - source pages 678-714
  • Chapter 25: Meta-Analyses, Systematic Reviews, and Evaluation Syntheses - source pages 715-740

Part 4: Use of Evaluation

  • Chapter 26: Pitfalls in Evaluations - source pages 745-776
  • Chapter 27: Writing Recommendations - source pages 777-790
  • Chapter 28: Writing Evaluation Reports - source pages 791-806
  • Chapter 29: Contracting for Evaluation Products and Services - source pages 807-840
  • Chapter 30: Evaluation in Government and the Politics of Evaluation - source pages 841-857
  • Chapter 31: Evaluation Challenges, Issues, and Trends - source pages 858-874

Legacy Extracts

  • Evaluation Planning and Design
  • Planning and Designing Useful Evaluations
  • Performance Measurement and Program Evaluation
